stories.what: Director Raj Nidimoru has revealed that Telugu superstar Chiranjeevi was the original choice to play the lead role in Amazon Prime Video's hit spy thriller The Family Man, before Manoj Bajpayee was eventually cast. The disclosure sheds light on how radically different the show's central character — and perhaps its entire tone — could have been. Bajpayee's portrayal of Srikant Tiwari went on to become one of Indian OTT's most celebrated performances.
stories.lens: For millions of The Family Man fans, this is a fascinating 'what if' — Chiranjeevi stepping into the role that made Manoj Bajpayee a pan-India OTT icon would have produced an almost unrecognisable show.
stories.article:
The Indian Express reports that filmmaker Raj Nidimoru, one half of the Raj & DK duo behind The Family Man, has revealed that Telugu megastar Chiranjeevi was the original choice for the lead role of intelligence officer Srikant Tiwari in the Amazon Prime Video series — a role that ultimately went to Manoj Bajpayee.

The casting revelation highlights how different the show's creative trajectory could have been. Chiranjeevi is one of the biggest stars in Telugu cinema, with a mass-hero screen presence quite distinct from Bajpayee's grounded, middle-class portrayal of Tiwari. The show, which premiered in 2019, became a landmark moment for Indian OTT content and ran for two successful seasons.

Manoj Bajpayee's performance as the harried, underpaid NIA officer balancing national security missions with a struggling domestic life earned widespread critical acclaim and multiple awards. The Family Man is widely credited with establishing the viability of high-budget, serialised spy drama on Indian streaming platforms.

For fans of both stars, the disclosure raises compelling questions about how the show's tone, reach, and identity might have differed had the original casting gone ahead — underscoring how pivotal a single casting decision can be to a series' legacy.
key_facts: Chiranjeevi was original choice for The Family Man's lead role
Manoj Bajpayee was eventually cast as Srikant Tiwari
Revealed by director Raj Nidimoru (Raj & DK)
The Family Man premiered on Amazon Prime Video in 2019

stories.headline: Haunted 3D Leads Weekend Box Office With Rs 13.35 Crore; Main Vaapas Aaunga and Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata Struggle
stories.what: The re-release of the 2011 horror film Haunted 3D has topped the domestic box office with a collection of Rs 13.35 crore, significantly outperforming two new Bollywood releases — Main Vaapas Aaunga and Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata — both of which struggled to find audiences. The result underscores the continued appetite for nostalgia-driven re-releases in Indian cinemas, a trend that has gained momentum over the past year.
stories.lens: When a 14-year-old horror film earns more than fresh Bollywood releases, it is a direct signal to producers and multiplex owners about where audience appetite — and risk — currently lies.
stories.article:
The Indian Express reports that the re-release of the 2011 Bollywood horror film Haunted 3D has claimed the top spot at the domestic box office, collecting Rs 13.35 crore and comfortably surpassing two new releases: Main Vaapas Aaunga and Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata, both of which failed to gain meaningful traction with audiences.

Haunted 3D, originally directed by Vikram Bhatt and starring Mahaakshay Chakraborty, was one of Bollywood's early forays into 3D horror. Its re-release performance continues a broader trend of Hindi film industry re-releases — including older Shah Rukh Khan, Aamir Khan, and Hrithik Roshan films — drawing strong crowds in recent months, suggesting audience nostalgia is a commercially viable force.

The underperformance of the new releases reflects ongoing struggles for mid-budget Bollywood films to compete for theatrical footfall. Multiplex chains and distributors have increasingly turned to re-releases and Hollywood imports to fill screens when new Hindi films fail to excite advance bookings.

For everyday moviegoers and the film trade alike, the box office outcome raises pointed questions about the viability of new Bollywood productions and why audiences are choosing older content over fresh offerings at the multiplex.
key_facts: Haunted 3D collected Rs 13.35 crore at the box office
Main Vaapas Aaunga and Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata both struggled
Haunted 3D is a re-release of the 2011 Vikram Bhatt horror film
Result continues trend of re-releases outperforming new Bollywood films

stories.headline: Drishyam 3 and Athiradi Lead South Indian OTT Releases This Week
stories.what: A fresh wave of South Indian films is arriving on OTT platforms this week, headlined by Drishyam 3 — the third instalment of the blockbuster Malayalam thriller franchise — alongside Tamil action film Athiradi and several other regional titles. Drishyam 3 is among the most anticipated OTT releases in recent months, given the enormous success of its predecessors starring Mohanlal.
stories.lens: Drishyam 3 streaming is the moment millions of Malayalam film fans have been waiting for — viewers who missed it theatrically can now watch one of the most eagerly anticipated sequels in Indian cinema from home.
stories.article:
Moneycontrol reports that this week's South Indian OTT slate is headlined by Drishyam 3, the third chapter in the landmark Malayalam thriller franchise, along with Tamil action film Athiradi and other regional releases now available for home streaming.

Drishyam 3 is one of the most closely watched OTT arrivals in recent memory. The franchise, featuring Mohanlal as Georgekutty, built a massive following across India through its first two instalments and set record numbers at the Kerala box office. The third film's digital release opens it up to the large number of viewers who follow the series on streaming platforms rather than theatres.

Athiradi is among the Tamil titles arriving alongside Drishyam 3, adding to a week that offers South Indian audiences a strong menu across Malayalam and Tamil languages. Other Kannada and Telugu titles are also part of the week's new OTT additions, as South Indian studios continue to feed a robust appetite for regional content on streaming services.

For OTT subscribers across India, the arrival of Drishyam 3 in particular marks a major streaming event — the culmination of a franchise that has been one of Indian cinema's most consistent crowd-pleasers over the past decade.
key_facts: Drishyam 3, third instalment of the Malayalam thriller franchise, now streaming
Tamil film Athiradi also among the week's OTT releases
Drishyam franchise stars Mohanlal as Georgekutty

stories.headline: Sanchita Ugale's Grandfather Breaks Down: 'She Had Work, No Godfather Needed'
stories.what: The grandfather of Sanchita Ugale, a young actor who recently came to public attention, broke down emotionally while speaking about her journey in the entertainment industry, saying she did not have a godfather and succeeded purely through her work. The emotional statement highlights the struggles faced by outsiders trying to break into Bollywood or the broader Indian entertainment industry without established industry connections.
stories.lens: In an industry where the nepotism debate is perennial, a grandfather's tearful defence of his granddaughter's merit is a deeply human story that resonates with every outsider trying to make it on their own.
stories.article:
The Indian Express reports that the grandfather of actor Sanchita Ugale broke down emotionally while speaking about her career, saying she did not have a godfather to support her rise in the entertainment industry and that her work alone defined her journey. The statement came as an emotional testament to the struggles faced by outsiders trying to build careers in Indian film and television without industry backing.

Sanchita Ugale is a young actor whose name has drawn recent public attention, and her grandfather's visible distress while vouching for her merit underscores the personal sacrifices families make alongside aspiring entertainers. The remark — 'Didn't have a godfather, she had work' — directly references the longstanding debate over nepotism in Bollywood and the wider Indian entertainment ecosystem.

The conversation around insider versus outsider privilege in Hindi film has been particularly charged since 2020, with audiences and industry observers paying close attention to how opportunities are distributed. Stories of artists who succeed without established connections continue to carry emotional weight with a public that largely identifies with the underdog.

For everyday viewers and aspiring artists alike, the grandfather's words serve as both a personal story of family pride and a pointed commentary on an industry where access and contacts have historically mattered as much as talent.
key_facts: Sanchita Ugale's grandfather broke down defending her career
Said she had no godfather and succeeded through her work alone
Story speaks to the outsider-versus-nepotism debate in Bollywood

stories.headline: Grammy Winner Ricky Kej: 'I'd Rather Be Lesser Known for Songs That Define Me'
stories.what: Grammy Award-winning Indian composer Ricky Kej, speaking at the Expresso event, said he would rather remain lesser known than compromise on music that truly defines him, signalling a principled stand against chasing mainstream popularity at the cost of artistic identity. Kej, who has won three Grammy Awards, is known for his work at the intersection of world music and environmental themes.
stories.lens: Ricky Kej is India's most-decorated musician at the Grammys, and his frank admission that artistic integrity matters more than fame offers a rare, candid window into how India's finest musicians navigate the tension between art and commerce.
stories.article:
The Indian Express reports that Grammy Award-winning Indian composer Ricky Kej, speaking at the Expresso event, stated that he would prefer to remain lesser known rather than make music that does not authentically represent him — a pointed articulation of artistic integrity over commercial appeal.

Kej, who has won three Grammy Awards, is one of India's most internationally recognised musicians. His work spans world music, ambient, and classical fusion, often centred on environmental and humanitarian themes. He has consistently operated outside Bollywood's commercial mainstream, building a global audience through albums that prioritise thematic depth over chart performance.

The remark reflects a broader conversation in Indian music about the pressures artists face to adapt their sound for mass-market streaming algorithms and film industry demand. For a musician of Kej's stature — recognised at the highest level of global music — the willingness to publicly prioritise identity over popularity carries notable weight.

For Indian music lovers and aspiring artists, Kej's statement is both an inspiration and a commentary on an industry where viral reach and film placements often define a musician's perceived success more than artistic vision.
key_facts: Ricky Kej has won three Grammy Awards
Said he prefers lesser fame over compromising his artistic identity
Kej is known for world music and environmentally themed compositions
